About the job


Google isn't just a software company. The Hardware Operations team is responsible for monitoring the state-of-the-art physical infrastructure behind Google's powerful search technology. As a Hardware Operations Manager, you will manage a team of Data Center Technicians. You will oversee the quality installation of server hardware and components and take charge of complicated installations/troubleshooting.

Your team will install, configure, test, troubleshoot and maintain hardware (like servers and its components) and server software (like Google's Linux cluster). They will also take on the configuration of more complex components such as networks, routers, hubs, bridges, switches and networking protocols. They may lead small project teams on larger installations and develop project contingency plans.

In this role, you will lead a team of Data Center Technicians who perform quality installations of server and network hardware and their components, and maintain and troubleshoot any tests while modeling teamwork with a positive impact to site culture. You will also manage day-to-day operations and guide the team through the installation, configuration, testing, troubleshooting and decommissioning of our servers. In a similar fashion, you will also oversee the deployment of network components of the data center, lead teams on installations and develop project contingency plans.

The AI and Infrastructure team is redefining what’s possible. We empower Google customers with breakthrough capabilities and insights by delivering AI and Infrastructure at unparalleled scale, efficiency, reliability and velocity. Our customers include Googlers, Google Cloud customers, and billions of Google users worldwide.

We're the driving force behind Google's groundbreaking innovations, empowering the development of our cutting-edge AI models, delivering unparalleled computing power to global services, and providing the essential platforms that enable developers to build the future. From software to hardware our teams are shaping the future of world-leading hyperscale computing, with key teams working on the development of our TPUs, Vertex AI for Google Cloud, Google Global Networking, Data Center operations, systems research, and much more.

Responsibilities

Lead a team of individuals, set and communicate individual and team priorities that support organizational goals. Meet regularly with individuals to discuss performance and development, and provide feedback and coaching.

Partner with teams to meet goals and stakeholders to manage facility activities and set/implement short and long-term strategies.

Maintain, monitor, and execute security and operational procedures and track/analyze trends to identify opportunities for improvements ensuring alignment with organizational policies.

Repair, fix, and perform preventative maintenance on equipment, servers, machines, or infrastructure based on identified issues.

Support and contribute to the implementation of Environmental Health and Safety (EHS) and other compliance programs and initiatives in collaboration with other teams to ensure environmental and safety incidents are investigated, resolved, and reported.

You’ll operate as a hands-on Product Owner and builder: translating user needs into roadmaps and backlogs, validating solutions through experiments, and shipping reliable automations and data pipelines that power lead generation, scoring, routing, nurturing, personalization, and measurement.

Job description:

1.Platform Strategy & Execution:

Own the product backlog for growth automations: translate business requirements into clear user stories, acceptance criteria, and prioritized sprints, aligned to the platform roadmap and OKRs.

Design and build workflow automations using Power Automate, Power Apps, Dataverse, and AI agents; extend with Python/SQL for custom connectors, data transformations, and APIs.

Implement observability (logging, alerting, run history, retries), SLAs, and versioned releases; ensure robust rollbacks and safe deployments for both low-code and Python assets.

Instrument and measure feature adoption and impact (e.g., lead velocity, conversion rate, cycle time, campaign throughput), and own cost-benefit tracking (licensing, compute) and P&L of platform capabilities.

Maintain platform reliability and advocate best practices (governance, standards, reusable components, documentation); proactively manage risk and technical debt.

Cross-Functional Collaboration & Data Optimization:

Partner with Data Operations, Data Science, Campaign Solution, and Marketing teams to deliver on-time solutions that meet functional requirements and non-functional needs (security, performance, scalability).

Act as the primary point of contact for operational issues, escalating and coordinating with the platform owner.

Streamline digital processes across the funnel with data governance, privacy, and integration standards in mind.

Champion data integrity and compliance across all customer data touchpoints.

Team Leadership & Capability Building:

Contribute to enablement and documentation to scale delivery across teams.

Participate in continuous training and development to build technical expertise and support ongoing platform enhancements.

Specialist Mentor teammates, lead discovery workshops, and guide solution design reviews; influence standards and architecture decisions.

Required Skills and Experience:

1. Technical Skills

Power Platform: Intermediate proficiency in Power Automate, Power Apps, and Dataverse; ability to build robust flows/apps with error handling, approvals, and connectors.

Python & SQL: Intermediate Python for APIs, data shaping, and integration. Basic-to-intermediate SQL for querying/transformations.

AI Agents: Practical experience designing or orchestrating task-oriented AI agents (prompting, grounding, evaluation, fallbacks, human-in-the-loop) within operations workflows.

Solid understanding of CRM and CMA systems, and how they integrate with data platforms and campaign tools.

Strong grasp of data management & governance.

Ability to translate sales/marketing needs into technical tasks within Agile/Scrum processes.

Familiarity with data visualization and/or campaign management platforms.

2.       Project and Stakeholder Management:

Proven ability to lead cross-functional delivery with clear communication, status reporting, and risk/issue management.

Experience in backlog prioritization, sprint planning, and roadmap execution with measurable outcomes.

Strong incident management and coordination skills to resolve operational issues quickly.

Ability to evaluate architectural impacts across business, information, and technical layers; comfortable with trade-off decisions.

3.       Experience Level:

Specialist: 3+ years of experience in MarTech/Marketing Ops/Growth Engineering or Product/Platform Engineering with quantified impact on lead generation, conversion, or operational efficiency.

Senior Associate: 1-3 years of relevant experience with demonstrated ability to deliver platform features, manage cross-team coordination, and maintain reliable operations.

Qualifications:

Experience with marketing automation, Power Platform, or customer engagement platforms.

Familiarity with cloud data platforms (e.g., Azure, Databricks) and data pipelines.

Hands-on exposure to AI/ML-driven automations.

Understanding of enterprise architecture frameworks, solution validation, and security/compliance practices.

Ideal Candidate Profile:

You’re a customer-obsessed technologist who loves to automate. You combine product thinking with hands-on building, using low-code + Python/SQL + AI agents to remove friction, accelerate growth, and prove impact with data. You thrive in fast-paced environments, collaborate well across teams, and balance speed with reliability, governance, and measurable outcomes.

HUMANICA Public Company Limited is seeking a talented Cybersecurity Engineer to join our team in Pathum Wan, Bangkok. This full-time position is crucial in ensuring the security and integrity of our organisation's digital infrastructure. As a Cybersecurity Engineer, you will play a key role in protecting our systems, data, and assets from cyber threats, while also driving innovation and implementing cutting-edge security solutions.

Job Summary:

We are seeking a Cybersecurity Engineer to safeguard our cloud-based platform. This role involves monitoring security operations, defending against threats, and ensuring compliance with regulatory standards (e.g., ISO 27001, SOC 2, PDPA/GDPR). You will work closely with DevOps, cloud infrastructure, and development teams to secure our Cloud-based environments and protect sensitive customer data.




Key Responsibilities:
Security Monitoring & Incident Response

Monitor Security Operations Center (SOC) alerts and respond to incidents in real-time.

Investigate and contain threats targeting web/mobile applications and cloud workloads.

Perform root cause analysis and coordinate incident response efforts.

Cloud Security & Infrastructure Hardening

Implement and manage cloud-native security controls (e.g., IAM policies, security groups, encryption).

Ensure secure configuration and monitoring of SaaS cloud environments (e.g., AWS/Huawei).

Review and enforce access controls across infrastructure and development pipelines.

Application Security (Web/Mobile)

Work with developers to integrate secure coding practices and perform code reviews.

Support Dynamic (DAST) and Static (SAST) Application Security Testing tools in CI/CD pipelines.

Identify and remediate vulnerabilities affecting SaaS components (e.g., APIs, authentication flows).

Endpoint & Identity Protection

Oversee endpoint security solutions for laptops, mobile devices, and remote workforces.

Support multi-factor authentication (MFA), Single Sign-On (SSO), and Role-Based Access Control (RBAC).

Compliance & Risk Management

Assist with the implementation of ISO 27001, SOC 2 Type 2, and local data protection laws (PDPA, GDPR).

Maintain documentation and evidence for audit readiness and customer trust programs.

Perform risk assessments, participate in third-party vendor security reviews, and support privacy-by-design.

Threat Intelligence & Reporting

Stay up to date with threats impacting the SaaS and HR/payroll landscape.

Analyze logs using SIEM tools and provide insights for proactive defense.

Generate monthly security reports for leadership and customers.

Qualifications:
Education & Certifications

Bachelor's degree in Cybersecurity, Information Technology, Computer Science, or a related field.

Industry certifications such as CompTIA Security+, SSCP, or ISC2 Certified in Cybersecurity.

Experience

1–2 years in cybersecurity, preferably in SaaS, fintech, or application environments.

Experience securing AWS/Huawei environments and containerized applications (e.g., Docker, Kubernetes).

Hands-on knowledge of SIEM, endpoint protection, vulnerability scanners, and cloud security tools.

Team Lead / Tech Lead (Full Stack)

Experience Level: Senior / Lead (6-8+ Years)

Job Description

As a Tech Lead, you will serve as the bridge between product management and engineering. You will be responsible for the technical vision, architectural integrity, and successful delivery of our software products. You will not only contribute high-level code but also mentor a team of developers, conduct code reviews, and define the engineering standards that ensure our systems are scalable, secure, and maintainable.

Key Responsibilities

● Technical Leadership: Define the technical roadmap and architectural patterns for the team. Make critical decisions on library selection, system design, and infrastructure.

● Team Mentorship: Lead and coach a team of developers, fostering a culture of continuous learning and high performance through pair programming and constructive code reviews.

● Architectural Oversight: Ensure the backend (Java/Spring Boot) and frontend (React/Vue) architectures remain decoupled, scalable, and optimized for performance.

● Stakeholder Collaboration: Work closely with Product Owners and Business Analysts to translate complex business requirements into technical specifications and manageable tasks.

● Process Excellence: Drive the adoption of Agile methodologies, CI/CD automation, and robust documentation practices to improve team velocity and code quality.

● Crisis Management: Act as the final point of escalation for complex technical issues and production bottlenecks.

Qualifications & Skills Required

● Education: Bachelor’s Degree or higher in Computer Engineering, Computer Science, or a related field.

● Experience: 6-8+ years in software development, with at least 2 years in a leadership or senior mentoring capacity.

The Frontend Test Engineer is responsible for the site-level technical strategy, implementation, and optimization of "Frontend" inspection and test solutions, including SPI, AOI, AXI/X-Ray, ICT, and Flying Probe. This role ensures high First Pass Yield (FPY), zero customer escapes, and minimal False Call Rates (FCR) through advanced data correlation and process control. The engineer will act as a technical lead, driving efficiency and quality across all frontend platforms.

Key Responsibilities:
1. Integrated Test & Inspection Strategy

Design and implement a cohesive "Frontend" test flow, ensuring optimal coverage overlap between SPI (Solder Paste Inspection), AOI (Automated Optical Inspection), and AXI (Automated X-Ray Inspection) to catch defects at the earliest possible stage.

Develop and debug complex test programs for ICT (e.g., Keysight 3070, Teradyne) and Flying Probe (e.g., Takaya) platforms.

2. Advanced Process Control & Optimization

Lead the False Call Rate (FCR) Reduction Program, specifically tuning AOI and AXI algorithms to eliminate "ghost defects" and reduce unnecessary repair loop waste.

Utilize SPI data to feed-forward information to SMT and AXI/AOI stations, creating a closed-loop system for real-time process adjustment and yield improvement.

Perform correlation analysis between System Integration Test (SIT) failures and Frontend inspection data to tighten test limits and prevent customer escapes.

3. Troubleshooting & Technical Lead

Act as the lead technical investigator for complex PCBA failures, using X-Ray/AXI to identify hidden defects like BGA head-in-pillow, voiding, or internal shorts.

Maintain a standardized digital repair database and technical troubleshooting guides to ensure a rapid Mean Time To Repair (MTTR) across the department.

Support the site goal of bonepile reduction by developing recovery procedures and optimized test routines for "difficult-to-debug" high-value assemblies.

4. Equipment Management & Continuous Improvement

Oversee the maintenance, calibration, and GR&R (Gage Repeatability and Reproducibility) for all frontend inspection systems (SPI/AOI/AXI).

Provide technical guidance and advanced training to technicians and junior engineers on equipment operation and programming best practices.

Lead Kaizen projects to automate manual data extraction and reporting, contributing to site-wide cost-saving and efficiency targets

Qualifications:

Education: Bachelor’s degree in Electrical, Electronics, or Mechatronics Engineering.

Experience: Minimum 5–8 years of hands-on experience in PCBA testing, with at least 3 years focused on AOI/AXI/SPI programming and optimization in a high-volume manufacturing environment.

Technical Skills:

Expert proficiency in AOI/AXI algorithm tuning and SPI volume thresholding.

Strong background in ICT (Keysight/Teradyne) and Flying Probe hardware and software.

Advanced knowledge of IPC-A-610 and IPC-7095 standards for inspection.

Ability to interpret complex Schematics, Gerber files, and CAD data for DFT/DFI analysis.

Experience with Statistical Process Control (SPC) and data visualization tools (e.g., Power BI/Minitab).

Soft Skills:

Proven ability to lead technical teams under "Line Down" or high-pressure situations.

Excellent analytical skills for data correlation across multiple test platforms.

Effective communication skills in English and local language for technical documentation and business reviews.

Job_Title: One Bangkok : Assistant Manager, Smart Facilities Management

Full_Description:
Overview:

We are seeking an Assistant Manager to lead the implementation of digital platform solutions for facilities management in One Bangkok. This role involves creating digital twins of buildings, infrastructure, MEP and technology systems to enhance operational efficiency, asset performance, sustainability, and user experience across the One Bangkok portfolio.

The ideal candidate will combine strong administrative and leadership skills with a deep understanding of digital solutions in facilities management. You will drive the digital experience strategy, ensuring technology adoption supports our mission of smarter, more connected facilities.




Key Responsibilities:

Develop and deploy digital twin models for buildings, common areas, and infrastructure assets.

Integrate building systems (HVAC, lighting, energy, security, etc.) with IoT sensors and facility management platforms.

Use BIM (Building Information Modeling) and GIS data to build accurate 3D/semantic models of estates.

Monitor building performance in real-time to drive predictive maintenance, energy optimization, and space utilization insights.

Collaborate with facilities teams, property managers, and IT teams to ensure seamless project implementation.

Support sustainability initiatives through data-driven building operations (e.g., carbon tracking, energy benchmarking).

Maintain digital twin fidelity over time with continuous data updates and system integrations.

Assist in preparing reports, presentations, and other relevant materials for management meetings and strategic planning sessions.

Stay informed on industry trends, emerging technologies, and best practices in smart facilities and digital experience management.




Qualifications & Preferred Skills:

Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Facilities Management, Engineering, Computer Science, or a related field.

3-5 years’ experience with BIM software (e.g., Autodesk Revit, Navisworks), IoT platforms, and digital twin frameworks (e.g., Azure Digital Twins, Siemens, Bentley).

Understanding of building operations, smart building technologies, and FM tools (e.g., IWMS, CAFM systems).

Knowledge of real estate lifecycle, asset management, and sustainability standards (e.g., LEED, ISO 50001).

Strong project management and stakeholder communication skills.

Previous work on smart building or campus-scale digital twin projects.

Familiarity with data analytics and visualization tools (e.g., Power BI, Tableau, Digital Twin dashboards).

Awareness of cybersecurity and data governance in smart environments

Ability to work independently and collaboratively in a team-oriented environment.

Job Purpose

Design and implement processes and ensure governance measurement in place across all IT functional units. Ensure that control framework is aligned to the organization direction. Provide dashboards for monitoring, analyzing, governing, improving the performance of IT with primary focus on strategic financial and non-financial IT department, enterprise goals by ensuring strategic projects testing is efficiency managed


Key responsibilities: 

Focus on the improvement and implementation of strategic, financial and non-financial goals

Develop and track plans for IT budgeting monitoring spends to be effective and within overall approval

Enable smooth and efficient IT purchases and implement methodical vendor management. Maintain good relationship with preferred vendors and provide evaluation for improvement

Participate in and play a part of the enterprise transformation program. Drive out required outputs to move along with the new IT initiatives and business case justifications

Ensure same processes are introduced and followed by resources in the same IT department after the amalgamation

Govern production release process management, policies and procedures. Coordinate with auditors for internal and external annual auditing exercises.

Ensure Testing COE deliver projects on time within budget with low defects to support operation efficiency and deliver fast time to market.


Qualifiations:

Master’s Degree in IT Management or related field.

More than 10 years of experience in working on IT Budgeting, Variance Analysis with demonstrated capability and track record in the practice of Planning & Performance Management.

Experience in Insurance domain is preferred.

Job_Title: Solution Customer Success Manager (SCSM) - SAP Business Data Cloud (Thailand)
Full_Description:
We help the world run betterAt SAP, we keep it simple: you bring your best to us, and we'll bring out the best in you. We're builders touching over 20 industries and 80% of global commerce, and we need your unique talents to help shape what's next. The work is challenging – but it matters. You'll find a place where you can be yourself, prioritize your wellbeing, and truly belong. What's in it for you? Constant learning, skill growth, great benefits, and a team that wants you to grow and succeed. 

We help the world run better

At SAP, we enable you to bring out your best. Our company culture is focused on collaboration and a shared passion to help the world run better. How? We focus every day on building the foundation for tomorrow and creating a workplace that embraces differences, values flexibility, and is aligned to our purpose-driven and future-focused work. We offer a highly collaborative, caring team environment with a strong focus on learning and development, recognition for your individual contributions, and a variety of benefit options for you to choose from.

Summary

The focus of this position is to help customers consume SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP) within their application projects as the cloud platform-of-choice. BTP is the engine for innovation through integration, extension, and analysis scenarios of the Intelligent Enterprise. SAP BTP CSPs work with the most cutting-edge and innovative SAP solutions and coach SAP’s most strategic customers.

The BTP Customer Success Partner (CSP) role is responsible for developing relationships with technical decision makers in their assigned accounts to ensure broad consumption of the portfolio both within and across application projects through Technical Advisory.

Responsibilities

You will proactively partner with customers to sustainably increase consumption of SAP BTP services, and coordinate activities with the Account Team.
You analyze the big picture, have expertise with digital transformation, and coach on technical and business architectures.
You are the primary SAP BTP strategic point of contact and will orchestrate success resources across SAP and customers, as described in the SAP BTP engagement model.
You will analyze your customer’s technical needs and execute consumption plans for your assigned customers.
You will provide recommendations and prepare the customer to make the best use of the solution, in alignment with SAP strategy and roadmap.
You will be a voice of the customer to internal SAP stakeholders.
You will work with colleagues from a broad variety of groups such as Cloud Success Services life-cycle relevant centers of experts, Account Teams, Pre-sales, Product Management groups, Support, Services, Partners, etc.
A BTP-CSP Advisor may also be assigned to fulfill a customer’s premium subscription services.

Our Team

We pride on a work environment that is inclusive and diverse. Every idea and perspective are valued.

The SAP Business Technology Platform CSP team is committed to sustain each other to ensure success, both individually and as a team. We have a pay-it-forward mentality and are always willing to help our colleagues. Our culture is built upon sharing best practices, utilizing communication platforms to answer each other’s questions, and bringing an entrepreneurial spirit to the mission of driving SAP BTP consumption growth together. We provide opportunities to lead regional and global workstreams that help us improve as a team and contribute to great customer experiences.

Role Requirements

You will excel in this role if:

You have 4-6 years of experience working in the IT Industry. Your background could be Software Engineering, Solution Management, Project/Product Management, Project Implementation, Pre-sales, Technical Customer Engagement, Customer Success, Proactive Support Services, Consulting, etc.
You have deep understanding of one or more areas of the SAP BTP portfolio: SAP BTP Extension and Integration Suite, Cloud Databases, Cloud Data Management, Analytics Cloud Solutions.
You have foundational knowledge of public cloud platforms and providers (e.g. Microsoft Azure, Amazon Web Services, Google Cloud, etc.) Certifications in enterprise architecture are a plus.
You have great communication and presentation skills. Familiarity with design thinking methodology is a plus. 
You can navigate complex organizational dynamics both internally at SAP, as well as externally with customers and partners.
You can continuously learn and adapt to the unique business and cultural practices of each region/country. 
You have a focus on customer service mindset and customer empathy.
You have a passion for learning new technologies and enjoy problem solving.

Bring out your bestSAP innovations help more than four hundred thousand customers worldwide work together more efficiently and use business insight more effectively. Originally known for leadership in enterprise resource planning (ERP) software, SAP has evolved to become a market leader in end-to-end business application software and related services for database, analytics, intelligent technologies, and experience management. As a cloud company with two hundred million users and more than one hundred thousand employees worldwide, we are purpose-driven and future-focused, with a highly collaborative team ethic and commitment to personal development. Whether connecting global industries, people, or platforms, we help ensure every challenge gets the solution it deserves. At SAP, you can bring out your best.  

Please note that any violation of these guidelines may result in disqualification from the hiring process.

Job Summary

The Senior Solution Architect plays a key role in defining, reviewing, and governing high-level solution architecture to ensure alignment with the organization’s strategic direction, cloud architecture standards (such as Azure, AWS, and GCP), and end-to-end system integration principles—covering both internal systems and external interfaces.

This role requires close collaboration with various cross-functional teams, including infrastructure, platform, database, DevOps, security, data, development, network, and other related teams. The goal is to co-design and deliver well-aligned architecture solutions that consider technical constraints and project timelines.

The architect is also responsible for ensuring that all solution designs conform to the enterprise architecture roadmap, architectural principles, best practices, and standards defined by both SCB TechX and its customers. Regular architecture reviews must be conducted to validate design quality, identify gaps, and work alongside Enterprise Architects to analyze domain-level challenges and propose continuous improvements.

Strong presentation and communication skills are essential for this role, as it involves articulating architectural ideas to stakeholders and delivering clear proposals and design solutions in enterprise architecture meetings and committees.

Job Responsibilities

Architecture Development

Design solution architecture in alignment with business needs, architecture roadmap, and organizational standards.

Deliver end-to-end architecture design, covering conceptual design, high-level architecture, detailed application design, infrastructure, and cloud systems.

Collaborate with relevant teams such as Infrastructure, Platform, Database, DevOps, Security, Development, Network, and Data to ensure effective solution design within technical and time constraints.

Ensure solution designs support complete end-to-end system integration, both internal and external.

Identify and apply appropriate integration patterns and document them in the reference architecture for future reuse.

Review solution delivery outcomes to identify design gaps and propose improvements to the architecture.

Maintain and manage architectural documentation and knowledge in the centralized architecture repository.

Project Collaboration

Support architectural activities throughout the project lifecycle—from planning and design to development and delivery.

Advise project teams to ensure alignment with enterprise architecture direction and cloud adoption practices.

Estimate infrastructure costs based on architectural designs to support project planning and budgeting.

Technology Evaluation and Adoption

Evaluate and recommend suitable technologies or approaches based on business goals, technical feasibility, and long-term maintainability.

Coordinate with technology vendors to gain a deep understanding of products and services.

Promote the reuse of software assets or solutions developed from previous projects to enhance operational efficiency.

 Background & Experience

Minimum of 5 years of experience in solution architecture design

Minimum of 8 years of experience in software development or related technical roles

Hands-on experience with designing and implementing cloud-based architecture (e.g., Azure, AWS, or GCP)

Experience in designing and managing end-to-end system integration with both internal and external systems

Experience working with cross-functional teams, including infrastructure, DevOps, security, data, and development.




Knowledge & Skills

Strong technical knowledge of enterprise-level application and infrastructure architecture

Ability to understand business requirements and translate them into appropriate architectural solutions

Solid understanding of enterprise architecture principles and reusable design patterns

Familiarity with modern architectural models such as microservices, serverless, event-driven, and API-first approaches

Knowledge of integration patterns and best practices in multi-system environments

Understanding of cloud-native services, DevOps practices, and infrastructure-as-code (IaC) concepts

Familiarity with architecture documentation and architecture repository tools for maintaining centralized architectural knowledge

Adastra TH Culture Manifesto

Servant Leadership

Managers are servants to employees. Managers are elected to make sure that employees have all the processes, resources, and information they need to provide services to clients in an efficient manner. Any manager up to the CEO is visible and reachable for a chat regardless their title. Decisions are taken with a consent in an agile manner and executed efficiently in no overdue time. We accept that wrong decisions happen and we appreciate the learning before we adjust the process for a continuous improvement. Employees serve clients. Employees listen attentively to client needs and collaborate internally as a team to cater to them. Managers and employees work together to get things done and are accountable to each other. Corporate KPIs are transparently reviewed on monthly company events with all employees. 

Performance Driven Compensation

We recognize and accept that some of us are more ambitious, more gifted, or more hard-working. We also recognize that some of us look for a stable income and lesser hassle at a different stage of their careers. There is a place for everyone, we embrace and need this diversity. Grades in our company are not based on number of years of experience, they are value driven based on everyone’s ability to deliver independently their work to clients and/or lead others. There is no “anniversary/annual” bonus, we distribute bonuses on a monthly recurring basis as an instant gratification for performance and this bonus is practically unlimited. There is no “annual indexation” of salaries, you may be upgraded several times within the year, or none, based on your own pace of progress, ambitions, relevant skillset and recognition by clients.

Work-Life Integration

We challenge the notion of work-life balance, we embrace the notion of work-life integration instead. This philosophy looks into our lives as a whole where we serve ourselves, our families and our clients in an integrated manner. We encourage 100% flexible working hours where you arrange your day. This means you are free when you have little work, but this also means extra effort if you are behind schedule. Working on a Western project also means nobody bothers you during the whole day but you may have to jump on a scrum call in the evening to talk to your team overseas. We appreciate time and we minimize time spent on Adastra meetings. We are also a remote-first company. While we have our collaboration offices and social events, we encourage people to work 100% remote from home whenever possible. This means saving time and money on commute, staying home with elderly and little ones, not missing the special moments in life. This also means you can work from any of our other offices in Europe, North America or Australia, or move to a place with lower cost of living without impacting your income. We trust you by default until you fail our trust. 

Global Diversity

Adastra Thailand is an international organization. We hire globally and our biggest partners and clients are in Europe, North America and Australia. We work on teams with individuals from different culture, ethnicity, sexual preference, political views or religion. We have zero tolerance to anyone who doesn’t pay respect to others or is abusive in any way. We speak different languages to one another, but we speak English when we are together or with clients. Our company is a safe space where communication is encouraged but boundaries regarding sensitive topics are respected. We accept and converge together to serve our teams and clients and ultimately have good time at work.

Lifelong Learning

On annual average we invest 25% of our working hours to personal development and upskilling outside project work, regardless of seniority or role. We feature more than 400 courses on our Training Repo and we continue to actively purchase or tailor hands-on content. We certify people on our expense. We like to say we are technology agnostic; we learn the principles of data management and we apply it on different use cases and different technology stacks. We believe that the juniors today are the seniors tomorrow, we treat everyone with respect and mentor them into the roles they deserve. We encourage seniors to give back to the IT community through leadership and mentorship. On your last day with us we may give you an open dated job offer so that you feel welcome to return home as others did before you. 

More About Adastra: 

Visit http://adastragrp.com and/or contact us: HRTH@adastragrp.com

Full_Description:
Job Summary
The Network Engineer is responsible for designing, implementing, maintaining, and supporting the organization’s network infrastructure across manufacturing and office environments. This role ensures secure, reliable, and high-performance connectivity that supports production systems, enterprise applications, and business operations. The Network Engineer will collaborate with IT teams, plant operations, and external vendors to ensure stable network performance and continuous improvement of network systems.

Key Responsibilities

Network Design and Implementation

Design, deploy, and maintain network infrastructure including LAN, WAN, WLAN, VPN, and cloud connectivity.
Plan network architecture to support manufacturing systems, enterprise applications, and plant operations.
Install and configure network hardware such as routers, switches, firewalls, and load balancers.

Network Administration and Monitoring

Monitor network performance, availability, and security using network monitoring tools.
Identify and troubleshoot network issues to minimize downtime and ensure optimal performance.
Manage IP addressing, VLAN configuration, routing, and switching.

Network Security Management

Implement network security measures including firewalls, intrusion detection and prevention systems (IDS/IPS), VPNs, and access controls.
Ensure compliance with company security policies and industry standards.
Support cybersecurity initiatives and maintain secure network access for internal and external users.

Maintenance and System Upgrades

Perform regular maintenance, patching, and upgrades of network hardware and software.
Maintain network documentation including network diagrams, configurations, and inventory.
Ensure high availability and reliability of network infrastructure.

Collaboration and Technical Support

Work closely with IT teams, system administrators, and plant operations to support enterprise and manufacturing applications.
Provide technical support and guidance for network-related projects and system implementations.
Assist end users and internal teams in resolving network connectivity issues.

Disaster Recovery and Business Continuity

Support the development and implementation of network disaster recovery and business continuity plans.
Ensure backup systems and failover mechanisms are properly configured and tested.

Vendor and Asset Management

Coordinate with vendors and service providers for procurement, maintenance, and support of network equipment.
Track and manage network assets and lifecycle management.

Continuous Improvement

Evaluate network performance and identify opportunities to enhance system efficiency, reliability, and security.
Implement best practices and new technologies to improve network operations.

Qualifications

Education

Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Information Technology, Network Engineering, or a related field.

Experience

Minimum 3–5 years of experience in Network Engineering or Network Administration.

Experience working in manufacturing or industrial environments is an advantage.

Experience supporting enterprise-scale network infrastructure.

Technical Skills

Strong knowledge of network protocols such as TCP/IP, BGP, OSPF, VLANs, and MPLS.

Hands-on experience with network devices including Routers, Switches, Firewalls, and Load Balancers.

Familiarity with network monitoring tools and troubleshooting methodologies.

Knowledge of network security principles, VPN technologies, and access control.

Experience with cloud networking platforms such as AWS, Microsoft Azure, or Google Cloud is a plus.

Preferred Certifications (Optional)

CCNA, CCNP, or other relevant networking certifications are an advantage.

About the Role: We are looking for a Head of Software Development to lead our entire engineering organization. In the brokerage and financial services world, software isn't just a support function; it is the business. Downtime during market hours isn't just an inconvenience— it's immediate financial loss and regulatory risk.

This role bridges the gap between our business strategy (e.g., launching new asset classes like structured notes or private markets, expanding offshore, improving trader experience) and technical execution. You will be responsible for building a resilient engineering culture, scaling and empowering our teams, and ensuring we deliver robust systems that handle extreme market volatility. Crucially, you will lead the strategic modernization of our existing platforms while ensuring our legacy systems remain bulletproof.

Key Responsibilities:

Strategic Technical Leadership & Business Alignment: Partner directly with the executive team to translate business goals into a concrete engineering roadmap. Leverage your deep business knowledge to know when to build custom matching engines, when to integrate vendors, and how technical decisions impact the bottom line.

Legacy System Modernization & Maintenance: Successfully balance two critical mandates: keeping our legacy financial systems highly stable and performant, while executing a strategic, phased modernization roadmap to transition them to modern architectures without disrupting daily trading operations.

Engineering Culture & Domain-Driven Design (DDD): Drive the technical standards for the company. Champion Domain-Driven Design (DDD) to accurately map complex financial business rules into our software architecture. Enforce rigorous testing, secure coding practices, and deployment pipelines that protect us from shipping bugs into production.

Team Hiring & Empowerment: Manage, mentor, and grow a diverse organization of software engineers and Tech Leads. You are responsible for raising the talent bar through strategic hiring, resource allocation, and building a culture where engineers are empowered to make architectural decisions and take ownership of their domains.

Flawless Delivery & Execution: Oversee the end-to-end delivery lifecycle. Adapt Agile methodologies to fit the strict release management requirements of a regulated financial institution.

Risk, Security, & Compliance Collaboration: Work hand-in-hand with our Risk, Compliance, and InfoSec teams. Ensure our architecture inherently supports regulatory reporting (e.g., SEC, local financial authorities) and adheres to strict data privacy frameworks.

Qualifications:

Experience: 10+ years of hands-on software engineering experience, with at least 5+ years leading multiple teams in a senior management capacity.

Domain Expertise & Business Acumen: Mandatory deep business knowledge in FinTech, Capital Markets, Brokerage, or high-volume transactional platforms. You must possess a strong understanding of diverse asset classes, specifically onshore and offshore equities, structured notes, mutual funds, and private markets. You must understand the financial mechanics behind the software (order routing, clearing, settlement across different jurisdictions) and speak the language of our business stakeholders natively.

Technical Chops & Core Stack: Deep background in designing distributed, highly available systems using Domain-Driven Design (DDD). You must have strong architectural experience with our core stack: Golang, .NET Core, SQL Server, and PostgreSQL.

Modernization Experience: Proven track record of maintaining complex legacy systems while successfully migrating them to modern, scalable microservices or event-driven architectures.

Communication: Exceptional ability to translate complex technical debt or architectural trade-offs into plain English for non-technical executives and board members

Full_Description:
About Us

Founded in 2012, ByteDance's mission is to inspire creativity and enrich life. With a suite of more than a dozen products, including TikTok, Lemon8, CapCut and Pico as well as platforms specific to the China market, including Toutiao, Douyin, and Xigua, ByteDance has made it easier and more fun for people to connect with, consume, and create content.​

Why Join ByteDance

Inspiring creativity is at the core of ByteDance's mission. Our innovative products are built to help people authentically express themselves, discover and connect – and our global, diverse teams make that possible. Together, we create value for our communities, inspire creativity and enrich life - a mission we work towards every day.​

As ByteDancers, we strive to do great things with great people. We lead with curiosity, humility, and a desire to make impact in a rapidly growing tech company. By constantly iterating and fostering an "Always Day 1" mindset, we achieve meaningful breakthroughs for ourselves, our Company, and our users. When we create and grow together, the possibilities are limitless. Join us.​

Diversity & Inclusion​

ByteDance is committed to creating an inclusive space where employees are valued for their skills, experiences, and unique perspectives. Our platform connects people from across the globe and so does our workplace. At ByteDance, our mission is to inspire creativity and enrich life. To achieve that goal, we are committed to celebrating our diverse voices and to creating an environment that reflects the many communities we reach. We are passionate about this and hope you are too.​

ResponsibilitiesAbout the Team

The Global Delivery Management team is mainly responsible for site selection, acquisition, and project delivery.

Preferred Qualifications

- Good relations with local telecommunication authorities & carriers will be a plus
- Certification as a Professional Engineer will be a plus

About the Hiring Team
Level Infinite is Tencent’s global gaming brand. It is a global game publisher offering a comprehensive network of services for games, development teams, and studios around the world.

We are dedicated to delivering engaging and original gaming experiences to a worldwide audience, whenever and wherever they choose to play while building a community that fosters inclusivity, connection, and accessibility. Level Infinite also provides a wide range of services and resources to our network of developers and partner studios around the world to help them unlock the true potential of their games.

What the Role Entails


We are looking for a Project Manager based in Bangkok to support product marketing initiatives for mobile and PC game titles in Thailand. This role focuses on coordinating marketing campaigns, game launches, and live-service updates by aligning cross-functional teams and ensuring smooth execution. The ideal candidate is highly organized, proactive, and experienced in managing marketing-driven projects in fast-paced environments..


Job Description:

Manage end-to-end execution of product marketing projects, including game launches, updates, live-service campaigns, and seasonal events.
 
Coordinate cross-functional teams such as marketing, creative, community, PR, and external agencies to ensure alignment and timely delivery.
 
Develop and maintain detailed project plans, timelines, and milestones for marketing initiatives.
 
Track project progress, identify risks or dependencies, and proactively resolve issues to keep campaigns on schedule.
 
Support product marketing teams by managing deliverables, approvals, and asset readiness across channels.
 
Ensure marketing campaigns align with product positioning, messaging, and global and regional brand guidelines.
 
Lead post-campaign reviews and documentation to capture insights and improve future executions.
Who We Look For

3+ years of experience in project management, preferably supporting product marketing, publishing, or campaign-based teams.
 
Experience working on marketing campaigns, product launches, or live-service initiatives.
 
Strong organizational and time management skills with the ability to manage multiple projects simultaneously.
 
Experience using project management tools is a plus.
 
Excellent communication and stakeholder management abilities.
 
Experience in gaming, mobile apps, or digital products is highly preferred.
Equal Employment Opportunity at Tencent

About Pandora Crafting & Supply
Pandora started crafting its jewellery in Thailand in 1989, where our founders fell in love with the delicate craftsmanship of the Thai people. To date, there are two crafting facilities in Thailand, Bangkok, and Lamphun, and we employ more than 13,000 skilled craftspeople. By the end of 2023, there are 107 million pieces sold globally, all of which were hand-finished here in Thailand by our skilled craftspeople using high-quality raw materials and sustainable methods.


About Pandora Group
Pandora is the world’s largest jewellery brand. Designed in Denmark and crafted in Thailand, Pandora jewellery provides endless opportunities for personalisation, allowing people to express their passions and show who they are. The company’s products are made from high-quality materials and sold in more than 100 countries through 6,700 points of sale, including 2,600 concept stores.

Pandora is committed to leadership in sustainability and is sourcing lab-grown stones and recycled silver and gold for all its jewellery. Headquartered in Copenhagen, Denmark, Pandora employs 33,000 people worldwide, is listed on the Nasdaq Copenhagen stock exchange, and generated sales of DKK 28.1 billion (EUR 3.8 billion) in 2023.

Job_Title: Specialist - CSR Project Management (ประสบการณ์ด้านผ้าทอ) Base Oversea
Full_Description:
มีหน้าที่รับผิดชอบหลักในการบริหารโครงการหิ่งห้อย คราฟต์ สตูดิโอ (Hinghoi Craft Studio)  และดำเนินกิจกรรม CSR ให้สอดคล้องกับเป้าหมายของโครงการไฟฟ้าพลังน้ำ หลวงพระบาง รวมถึงนโยบายด้านความยั่งยืนของบริษัท โดยมีความรับผิดชอบสำคัญดังนี้:

1.       บริหารและกำกับโครงการหิ่งห้อย คราฟต์ สตูดิโอเชิงกลยุทธ์
วางแผนกลยุทธ์การดำเนินงาน แผนงาน งบประมาณ KPI และกรอบเวลา พร้อมติดตาม ประเมินผล และรายงานความก้าวหน้าให้เป็นไปตามเป้าหมายองค์กร

2.       พัฒนาอาชีพและเสริมสร้างศักยภาพชุมชนด้านงานหัตถกรรม
กำกับการฝึกอบรม ถ่ายทอดองค์ความรู้ จัด Workshop และ กิจกรรมพัฒนาทักษะรายสัปดาห์ (Clinic) ประสานความร่วมมือกับภาครัฐ สถานศึกษา ชุมชน และหน่วยงานที่เกี่ยวข้อง โดยอิงจากความต้องการของชุมชนรอบโครงการ

3.       บริหารการพัฒนาผลิตภัณฑ์และห่วงโซ่อุปทานงานหัตถกรรม
สนับสนุน การจัดหาวัตถุดิบ การผลิต การควบคุมคุณภาพ การรับซื้อจากชุมชน การกำหนดมาตรฐาน กำหนดราคา การบริหารรายการสินค้าเข้า-ออก การบันทึกบัญชีเบื้องต้น และกิจกรรมส่งเสริมการขาย

4.       บริหารทรัพยากร ทรัพย์สิน และการดำเนินงานของโครงการ
บริหารบุคลากร งบประมาณ ทรัพย์สิน และรายการสินค้าเข้า-ออกของโครงการ รวมถึงการเตรียมความพร้อมสถานที่ ข้อมูล การนำเสนอ และการต้อนรับผู้เยี่ยมชม

5.       สนับสนุนและขับเคลื่อนกิจกรรม CSR ภายใต้ CCSM – LPCL CSR
บริหารงบประมาณ CSR ประจำปี ประสานงานโครงการและจิตอาสา ตรวจสอบความสอดคล้องกับกลยุทธ์ความยั่งยืน จัดทำรายงานตามกรอบ CKPower CSR Strategy

6.       พัฒนาและรักษาความสัมพันธ์กับผู้มีส่วนได้ส่วนเสียทั้งภายในและภายนอกองค์กร รวมถึงหน่วยงานภาครัฐ ชุมชน และพันธมิตรทางธุรกิจ

7.       จัดทำรายงานผลการดำเนินงาน วิเคราะห์ และนำเสนอข้อมูลเพื่อการพัฒนาและปรับปรุงโครงการอย่างต่อเนื่อง

8.       บรรยายกิจกรรมโครงการหิ่งห้อย คราฟต์ สตูดิโอ ให้แก่คณะผู้เยี่ยมชมโครงการ รวมถึงหน่วยงานภายนอก

วิเคราะห์ปัญหาและความเสี่ยง พร้อมเสนอแนวทางการพัฒนาและปรับปรุงการดำเนินงานอย่างเป็นระบบ

·       จัดทำรายงานผลการดำเนินงานรายเดือนและรายปี เสนอต่อผู้บริหาร

ความรับผิดชอบด้าน หิ่งห้อย สตูดิโอ (CSR พัฒนางานฝีมือและการสร้างอาชีพ)

กำกับการพัฒนาความร่วมมือด้านการสร้างอาชีพ การฝึกอบรม และการถ่ายทอดองค์ความรู้ด้านหัตถกรรม

วางแผนและกำกับการดำเนินการจัดกิจกรรมพัฒนาทักษะงานฝีมือ และฝึกอาชีพด้านการทอผ้า ปักผ้า การเย็บจักร และงานหัตถกรรม (จัด Workshop อย่างน้อย 2-4 ครั้งต่อปี และ กำกับการดำเนินงานกิจกรรมพัฒนาทักษะรายสัปดาห์ (Clinic) ทุกวันเสาร์

ประสานงานกับหน่วยงานภาครัฐ สถานศึกษา ชุมชน และองค์กรที่เกี่ยวข้องเพื่อสนับสนุนการดำเนินโครงการ

ลงพื้นที่สำรวจและวิเคราะห์ความต้องการของชุมชุมในพื้นที่โดยรอบโครงการไฟฟ้าพลังน้ำ หลวงพระบาง

วางแผนและจัดการเยี่ยมชมโครงการสำหรับหน่วยงานภายนอก ผู้บริหาร และผู้ที่สนใจ

กำกับการจัดเตรียมข้อมูล สื่อ การนำเสนอ และความพร้อมของอาคารหิ่งห้อย สตูดิโอ

นำเสนอผลงาน ผลการดำเนินงาน และผลลัพธ์โครงการต่อผู้บริหาร หน่วยงานภายนอก และผู้มีส่วนได้เสีย

บริหารจัดการทรัพย์สินและรายการสินค้าเข้า-ออก ของโครงการ รวมถึง จัดหา ดูแลรักษา และการซ่อมบำรุง

ความรับผิดชอบด้าน หิ่งห้อย คราฟต์ (สินค้า การพัฒนาผลิตภัณฑ์ จัดหา รับซื้อ และจัดจำหน่ายผลิตภัณฑ์งานหัตถกรรม)

ร่วมดูแลและสนับสนุนการบริหารจัดการห่วงโซ่อุปทานของผลิตภัณฑ์หัตถกรรม ได้แก่: • จัดหาและสั่งผลิตวัตถุดิบ • ควบคุมคุณภาพสินค้า (QA/QC) •การคัดเลือกและประสานงานผู้ผลิต• จัดการการขนส่งและตรวจรับสินค้า • ประสานงานกับร้านตัดเย็บในหลวงพระบาง

ร่วมดูแลและสนับสนุนการรับซื้อผลิตภัณฑ์หัตถกรรมจากชุมชน และจัดการด้านการเงินร่วมกับชุมชนผู้ผลิต

ร่วมดูแลและร่วมกำหนดมาตรฐานคุณภาพ ราคา และการตรวจรับผลิตภัณฑ์จากชุมชน

ร่วมพัฒนาสื่อส่งเสริมการขายและจัดกิจกรรมทางการตลาด

บริหารจัดการรายการสินค้าเข้า-ออก และกำกับการจัดทำบัญชีเบื้องต้นของโครงการ

ความรับผิดชอบกิจกรรม CSR ภายใต้ CCSM - LPCL CSR

สนับสนุนการพัฒนาและขับเคลื่อนกลยุทธ์ CSR ของ LPCL ในภาพรวม

·       ตรวจสอบให้การดำเนินโครงการสอดคล้องและสนับสนุนเป้าหมายด้านความยั่งยืนของบริษัท

จัดทำและบริหารงบประมาณ CSR ประจำปี

ประสานงานกับผู้จัดการโครงการต่างๆ ในการดำเนินกิจกรรม CSR

สรรหาและประสานงานจิตอาสา รวมถึง CSR leader (project lead) สำหรับกิจกรรมภายใต้โครงการหิ่งห้อย คราฟต์ สตูดิโอ  

รวบรวมข้อมูลและจัดทำรายงานความยั่งยืนตามกรอบ CKPower CSR strategy framework และ GRI Standard หรือมาตรฐานแบบประเมินที่เกี่ยวข้อง

ประสานงานด้านโลจิสติกส์สำหรับผู้เข้าร่วมกิจกรรม เช่น ที่พัก อาหาร และการเดินทางของผู้เข้าร่วมกิจกรรม

การประสานงานข้ามสายงาน

ทำงานร่วมกับหน่วยงานสนับสนุนต่างๆ เพื่อให้การดำเนินโครงการเป็นไปอย่างราบรื่น:

o   SSC Env+ PVD:  การดำเนินงานร่วมกับชุมชนในการดำเนินโครงการ เช่น การจัดอบรมพัฒนาอาชีพให้กับชุมชน • ประสานงานนายบ้านในการดำเนินกิจกรรม CSR ร่วมกับชุมชน

o   SSC Admin: ดูแลอาคารหิ่งห้อย สตูดิโอ ทำความสะอาด ซ่อมบำรุงต่างๆ • ประสานงานหลักในเรื่องที่พัก รถรับ-ส่ง สำหรับทำกิจกรรมของโครงการ เช่น วิทยากร ชาวบ้านที่ร่วม Workshop ของโครงการ

o   SSC Procurement (Logistic): การจัดซื้อจัดจ้าง การนำเข้า-ส่งออก

o   SSC MDO: การเยี่ยมชมพื้นที่และการเตรียมสถานที่รับรองแขกของโครงการ

o   SSC Accounting: การบันทึกธุรกรรมของโครงการอย่างถูกต้อง การทำบัญชีรายรับ-รายจ่าย การทำเอกสารนำส่งภาษี

o   SCC Legal: ข้อกำหนดและเอกสารด้านกฎหมายที่เกี่ยวข้องกับกิจกรรมโครงการ

o   SSC Engineer: ประสานงานงานก่อสร้าง ซ่อมบำรุงอาคารเพิ่มเติม  (ร่วมกับ Architect, engineer)

Job Purpose:

To lead and deliver assigned projects and initiatives by coordinating cross-functional teams, ensuring timely execution, quality outcomes, and alignment with business objectives.

The role is responsible for driving continuous improvement, managing stakeholder expectations, and translating strategic directions into effective operational execution.

Job Description:

Overseeing all insurance strategic projects in project management pipeline to be delivered on-time, within scope and budget.

Coordinating with cross functional team and external parties for the flawless execution.

Building and implementing a Go-to-Market Strategy.

Coordinating with cross functional team to develop communication pack, selling materials, and other documents as needed to ensure successful implementation.

Aligning marketing programs, campaigns and operational activities to insurance annual plans and targets.

Developing a detailed project plan to monitor, track progress and escalate to management as needed.

Tracking project performance, specifically to analyze the successful completion of short and long-term goals.

Monitoring project deliverables to ensure that the project produce committed results.

Providing post-project evaluations and identifying successful and unsuccessful factors.

Delegating project tasks based on junior staff members' individual strengths, skill sets and experience levels.

Assisting Head of Auto Insurance in the preparation of project roadmap and plans to achieve financial targets both top line and net income.

Using and continually developing leadership skills.

Maintaining professional and technical knowledge.

Performing other related duties as assigned

Qualifications:

Bachelor’s degree in any related field.

At least 10 years of total working experience.

At least 5 years of experience in marketing/ project management/ product for Auto Insurance in non-life insurance company or broker.

Proven working experience in project management, auto insurance or marketing.

Strong working knowledge of Microsoft Office, especially Excel and Powerpoint.

Good in Communication and interpersonal skills with solid organizational skills including attention to detail and multitasking skills.

Excellent communication, interpersonal, and problem-solving skills.

Ability to coordinate and communicate effectively with stakeholders.

Fast learner with positive thinking and excellent presentation skills.

Strong organizational skills and ability to manage multiple projects simultaneously.

Full_Description:
1. วิเคราะห์ และออกแบบการทำงานของ Mobile Application

2. พัฒนา Mobile Application ทั้งบนระบบปฏิบัติการ iOS และ Android

3. แก้ไขข้อผิดพลาด (Bug) ของ Mobile Application ทั้งผลิตภัณฑ์เดิม และพัฒนาขึ้นใหม่

4. ปรับปรุงประสิทธิภาพการทำงานของ Mobile Application ที่มีอยู่ให้ดียิ่งขึ้น รวมถึงการอัพเดตเวอร์ชั่นของ Application และเครื่องมือให้สามารถใช้ได้กับ OS Version ใหม่ๆ

5. ทดสอบการใช้งาน Mobile Application และ WebService

6. ศึกษาค้นคว้าเทคโนโลยี และเทคนิคใหม่ๆ ในการทำงานเสมอ พร้อมทั้งเสนอ และแลกเปลี่ยนความรู้กันในทีมพัฒนา

7. ปฏิบัติงานอื่นๆตามที่ได้รับมอบหมาย

คุณสมบัติ

1. ปริญญาตรี ด้าน Information Technology, Computer Science, Computer Engineering หรือสาขาที่เกี่ยวข้อง

2. มีความรู้ Programming Language Flutter , Dart , Swift , Kotlin , .Net

3. มีความรู้ความเข้าใจใน Android OS, iOS Architecture เป็นอย่างดี

4. มีความเข้าใจในขั้นตอนการพัฒนา Mobile Application

5. มีความเข้าใจใน UX/UI และ มีทักษะในการวิเคราะห์และแก้ไขปัญหาทีดี

6. มีประสบการณ์ในการพัฒนา Mobile Application เชื่อมต่อกับ WebService (API)

7. หากมีประสบการณ์การพัฒนา Mobile Application ด้วย React Native จะพิจารณาเป็นพิเศษ

Job Description

1、Responsible for designing and optimizing the Top Seller Program, using logical thinking to create effective incentive mechanisms.
2、Possess structured problem-solving skills to address issues encountered during program execution and propose solutions.
3、Excellent verbal and written communication skills are required to coordinate resources and ensure smooth program implementation.
4、Strive for operational excellence to guarantee the efficient running of the program, enhancing seller satisfaction and participation.

Job Requirements

1、Bachelor's degree or above, with a preference for Marketing, Business Administration, or related majors.
2、At least 1 years of e-commerce platform operation experience, with a preference for those with top seller or similar project management experience.
3、Skilled in logical thinking and innovation, with a deep understanding of e-commerce platform operations.
4、Demonstrated team player with excellent communication and coordination skills in cross-functional collaboration.
5、Attention to detail, responsible, and able to maintain high operational efficiency in a fast-paced environment.

Key Responsibilities

Lead end-to-end project delivery with full accountability for overall project success

Define project strategy, scope, timeline, budget, and resource planning across initiatives

Drive project governance, methodologies, and standardization across the organization

Oversee project scheduling, resource allocation, and cross-functional coordination

Identify, assess, and mitigate risks, and manage changes throughout the project lifecycle

Establish and control project budgets, including contract and vendor management

Lead and manage multiple project teams and team leads across various workstreams

Monitor project performance, track progress, and ensure alignment with business objectives

Provide regular updates to senior management and escalate critical issues when required

Continuously improve PMO processes, tools, and best practices

Qualifications

Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Computer Science, IT, Computer Engineering, or related field

8–10 years of experience in project management (experience leading large-scale projects preferred)

Proven leadership experience managing cross-functional teams

Strong analytical thinking and problem-solving capabilities

Ability to work effectively under pressure in a fast-paced environment

Strong decision-making and stakeholder management skills

Hands-on experience with project management tools (e.g., Microsoft Project, Jira, Asana, Microsoft Teams)

Soft Skills

High ownership and accountability with a results-oriented mindset

Excellent communication skills with both business and technical stakeholders

Strong leadership, coaching, and team development capabilities

Ability to manage multiple parallel projects and priorities effectively

Certifications (Preferred)

PMP / Agile / Scrum / ITIL

Responsibilities

Facilitate Scrum events (Sprint Planning, Daily Stand-ups, Sprint Reviews, and Retrospectives) ensuring they are productive and kept within the timebox.

Coach the Development Team in self-management and cross-functionality.

Assist the Product Owner with effective Product Backlog management and techniques for backlog refinement.

Help the team understand the need for clear and concise Product Backlog items.

Facilitate collaborative planning in a complex environment.

Identify, track, and help remove impediments that prevent the team from reaching its Sprint goals.

Shield the team from outside interruptions and unmanaged "scope creep" during the Sprint.

Lead and coach the organization in its Scrum adoption.

Plan Scrum implementations within the organization.

Work with other Scrum Masters to increase the effectiveness of the application of Scrum in the organization.




Requirements

Bachelor’s degree or higher in Computer Science, Computer Engineer, Information Systems, or equivalent field of study and professional experience

Minimum of 5 years’ experience as an Scrum Master

Experience working with multiple projects at the same time

Experience managing project by using agile methodology

CSM or PSM Certification

Experience in managing third-party vendors in project implementation

Excellent communication in Thai and English, interpersonal skills, customer/business focused, result-oriented, and service mindset

Experience in Oil and Gas industry would be an advantage

We are seeking a highly skilled and experienced Lead Solution Engineer to act as a senior technical expert and mentor within our Field Sales support team. In this role, you will be instrumental in tackling complex customer challenges, designing innovative solutions, and delivering compelling demonstrations of the Freshworks platform. As a technical pillar for the team, you will guide and support other Solution Engineers, champion best practices, and partner closely with sales management to drive revenue growth by ensuring technical success across mid-market and enterprise customer engagements.

Key Responsibilities:

Technical Leadership & Mentorship:

Serve as a senior technical resource and subject matter expert for the Solution Engineering team and broader sales organization.

Mentor, coach, and provide guidance to other Solution Engineers on technical best practices, solution design, and demonstration skills.

Lead by example in complex deal support, setting a high bar for technical excellence.

Advanced Pre-Sales Engagement:

Work closely with our mid-market & enterprise customers to deeply understand their critical business problems and technical requirements.

Lead and conduct advanced discovery calls, architect sophisticated solutions, and deliver compelling, tailored product demonstrations and Proofs of Concept (POCs) for strategic and complex deals.

Expertly handle complex technical inquiries and objections from customers.

Solution Design & Delivery:

Drive user requirement gathering, in-depth analysis, and scope definition for complex projects.

Oversee and contribute to the creation of high-quality pre-sales documentation, including technical proposals, SoWs, wireframes, and RFP/RFI responses, ensuring technical accuracy and alignment with customer needs.

Internally qualify sales opportunities from a technical feasibility and solution-fit perspective, identifying potential risks and mitigation strategies.

Collaboration & Strategy:

Partner with sales management, providing expert advice on the effective use and deployment of technical resources for key opportunities.

Maintain close alignment and foster strong working relationships with Sales, Product Management, Engineering, Customer Support, and Implementation teams to ensure seamless customer experiences and provide valuable field feedback.

Contribute to the continuous improvement of pre-sales processes, tools, and knowledge sharing.

Job Summary

The SAP PP Consultant is responsible for the design, configuration, implementation, and support of the SAP Production Planning (PP) module within a manufacturing environment. This role works closely with production, planning, supply chain, and cross-functional teams to ensure SAP solutions effectively support production planning, material availability, capacity planning, and manufacturing execution in alignment with business requirements.

Key Responsibilities

SAP PP Implementation & Support

Configure and implement SAP PP processes, including:

Demand Management and Planned Independent Requirements (PIR)

Material Requirements Planning (MRP)

Bill of Materials (BOM)

Routing and Work Centers

Capacity Planning

Production Orders / Process Orders

Shop Floor Control (SFC)

Provide end-to-end support for SAP PP processes in a manufacturing environment, including incident handling and issue resolution.

Business Requirement Analysis

Analyze business requirements and translate them into functional SAP PP solutions.

Prepare functional specifications for enhancements, interfaces, reports, and forms related to production planning and manufacturing processes.

Business Process Integration

Collaborate with internal stakeholders to optimize Plan-to-Produce (P2P) and manufacturing processes.

Ensure seamless integration of SAP PP with other SAP modules such as:

MM (Materials Management)

QM (Quality Management)

PM (Plant Maintenance)

FI/CO (Finance & Controlling)

Support alignment between sales forecasts, production planning, and material availability.

Testing & Deployment

Conduct unit testing, integration testing, and user acceptance testing (UAT).

Support system go-live activities, data migration (BOM, Routing, Work Center, and MRP data), and cutover planning.

Provide post-go-live support and system stabilization.

User Support & Training

Provide day-to-day SAP PP system support and troubleshooting.

Train end users such as production planners and manufacturing teams.

Prepare user manuals and system documentation.

Act as a subject matter expert (SME) for SAP PP-related inquiries.

Continuous Improvement

Identify opportunities to improve manufacturing efficiency through SAP PP functionality.

Support system upgrades, enhancements, and rollouts to new plants or regions.

Ensure SAP PP solutions comply with internal controls and audit requirements.

Qualifications & Requirements

Education

Bachelor’s degree in Information Technology, Computer Science, Engineering, Industrial Engineering, Business Administration, or a related field.

Experience

Minimum 3–7 years of experience as an SAP PP Consultant, SAP Enterprise Integration Architect.

Hands-on experience with SAP PP in manufacturing or industrial environments.

Experience in at least one full-cycle SAP implementation project.

Strong understanding of manufacturing processes, MRP, and production planning concepts.

Strong knowledge of SAP PP configuration, including:

-          MRP parameters

Knowledge of integration between SAP PP and MM, QM, and FI/CO modules.

          1.4 ศึกษาและพัฒนางานด้านภูมิสารสนเทศ เพื่อรองรับงานเกี่ยวกับกฎระเบียบทางการค้า และมาตรการด้านสิ่งแวดล้อมในห่วงโซ่อุปทานข้าวโพดเลี้ยงสัตว์, ข้าว, มันสำปะหลัง, ปาล์มน้ำมันและถั่วเหลือง




2. งานด้านการประเมินพื้นที่เพาะปลูก และการประเมินผลผลิตพืช

          2.1 ติดตามและประเมินสถานการณ์การเพาะปลูกเกี่ยว (Crop Progress & Crop Monitoring) เช่น ความคืบหน้าของการเพาะปลูก, การเจริญเติบโต, การเก็บเกี่ยว โดยใช้ภาพถ่ายดาวเทียม เช่น NDVI, EVI, LAI เป็นต้น

          2.2 แปลพื้นที่เพาะปลูกพืชวัตถุดิบอาหารสัตว์จากข้อมูลภาพถ่ายดาวเทียม ในแหล่งเพาะปลูกหลักของโลก

          2.3 วิเคราะห์ความเปลี่ยนแปลงของพื้นที่เพาะปลูก (Land Use / Land Cover Change)

          2.4 พัฒนาระบบประเมินความสมบูรณ์ของพืช และประเมินผลผลิตพืชจากภาพถ่ายดาวเทียม

          2.5 ติดตามและประเมินผลกระทบจากน้ำท่วม

          2.6 วิเคราะห์แนวโน้มผลผลิตและคาดการณ์ผลผลิตระดับแปลง

          2.7 พัฒนาระบบงานด้านเทคโนโลยีภูมิสารสนเทศ

- สามารถใช้เครื่องมือวิเคราะห์ข้อมูลเชิงพื้นที่ (Spatial Analysis) ได้

          - มีความรู้เกี่ยวกับ Remote Sensing, การประมวลผลภาพถ่ายดาวเทียม และดัชนีพืชพรรณต่าง ๆ อาทิเช่น NDVI, EVI, LAI, NDWI, SAVI, VCI, NBR, NDSI

          - มีความสามารถในการแปล/จำแนกชนิดของพืชจากภาพถ่ายดาวเทียม Sentinel, Landsat, THEOS หรือดาวเทียมสำรวจทรัพยากรธรรมชาติ ที่มีรายละเอียดภาพสูง-รายละเอียดระดับกลาง

          - เข้าใจหลักการตรวจสอบย้อนกลับ (Traceability) และข้อมูลการเพาะปลูกด้านการเกษตร

Role Summary

As a Mid-Level Cloud Solutions Engineer, you will be responsible for the design, deployment, and optimization of highly available and secure cloud infrastructure on Google Cloud Platform (GCP). You will act as a technical owner, driving implementation and automation, and guiding clients through complex cloud transformation initiatives.

Key Responsibilities
Cloud Engineering & Deployment

Architecture & Implementation: Design, deploy, and manage secure, scalable, and cost-effective cloud infrastructure on GCP (Compute Engine, GKE, Cloud Run, Cloud Storage, VPCs, etc.).

Infrastructure as Code (IaC) Ownership: Develop, maintain, and version control infrastructure using IaC tools (e.g., Terraform or Google Cloud Deployment Manager) to ensure consistency and repeatability across environments.

DevOps and CI/CD: Implement and manage Continuous Integration/Continuous Deployment (CI/CD) pipelines (e.g., Cloud Build, Jenkins, GitLab) for automated application deployment and infrastructure changes.Implementing security-by-design principles.

Security: Implement and manage cloud security controls (e.g., IAM, network segmentation, encryption) and ensure compliance with relevant security policies and regulations including security vulnerabilities, incidents, and implement security-by-design principles.

Modernization & Migration: Lead or execute complex workload migrations from on-premises environments or other cloud platforms to GCP, minimizing downtime and implementing best practices.

Optimization & Operations

FinOps & Cost Optimization: Proactively identify and implement cost-saving measures and efficiency strategies (e.g., resource right-sizing, committed use discounts) to optimize client cloud expenditure.

Monitoring & Observability: Configure and manage monitoring, logging, and alerting systems (Google Cloud Operations Suite/Cloud Monitoring/Logging) to ensure high availability, performance, and security compliance.

Troubleshooting: Serve as an escalation point for complex infrastructure issues, performing deep-dive root cause analysis and implementing permanent fixes.

Consulting & Collaboration

Solution Validation: Provide expert technical consultation and solution validation to the sales and pre-sales teams, accurately scoping technical components and estimating effort.

Cross-Functional Partnership: Collaborate with development, data, and security teams to align infrastructure with application requirements and security policies.

Emerging Technologies: Maintain proficiency in cutting-edge GCP services and trends, including Generative AI services (e.g., Vertex AI) and serverless technologies.

Requirements

Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or a related technical field (or equivalent practical experience).

In-depth knowledge of Google Cloud Platform (GCP) services across Compute, Networking, Storage, and Security (IAM, VPC, Cloud Armor).

Proven experience (2+ years) designing, deploying, managing production cloud-based applications and infrastructure including writing security-conscious configurations.

Expertise in Infrastructure as Code (IaC) tools, preferably Terraform.

Strong hands-on experience with container technologies (Docker) and orchestration (Kubernetes / GKE).

Proficiency in Linux administration and shell scripting, with demonstrable skills in an automation language like Python, Go, or Bash.

Familiarity with database systems (RDBMS, NoSQL, BigQuery) and networking fundamentals (VPC, load balancing, DNS).

Proven experience implementing security controls such as IAM, network security (firewalls, WAF), and data encryption in a cloud environment with a strong focus on cloud security services and best practices.

Google Professional Cloud Engineer or Professional Cloud Architect Certificate is highly desirable (or equivalent certifications from AWS/Azure with a commitment to achieve GCP certification within a defined period).

Strong English communication skills (written and verbal) for technical documentation and international/client collaboration.

Proficient in Thai (or the local language) for internal and local client discussions.

Proven ability to work independently, manage workload, and act as a technical leader on project deliveries.

Impact Monitoring: Establish key performance indicators (KPIs) and monitoring frameworks to track the progress and impact of decarbonization initiatives, providing regular reports and recommendations for improvement.  

Collaboration Opportunities: Identify opportunities for cross-sector collaboration and knowledge sharing among clients, facilitating the exchange of best practices and fostering a community of sustainability leaders.  

Market Research: Conduct market research and analysis to identify emerging trends, technologies, and financing mechanisms relevant to industry/sector-specific decarbonization efforts, ensuring the bank remains at the forefront of sustainable finance.

Expertise in one or more specific industries/sectors (e.g., manufacturing, agriculture, transportation, etc.) and familiarity with sector-specific challenges, regulations, and decarbonization strategies

Project management experience, including the ability to manage multiple initiatives, prioritize tasks, and deliver results within deadlines

Familiarity with sustainable finance, green investment products, and relevant international standards and frameworks 

A self-motivated and proactive approach with a passion for sustainability and a commitment to driving positive environmental impact

Job Descriptions

Implement, enhance, and support SAP GRC products, IBM products, and in-house applications related to risk management.

Implement and support IBM OpenPages with Watson 8.3 (ORM).

Implement and support GRC Access Control and GRC Process Control for oil & gas clients across key processes: P2P, O2C, IT, H2R.

Execute Segregation of Duties (SOD) and Critical Access (CA) risk analysis.

Perform root cause analysis on SOD and CA issues identified by GRC Access Control.

Analyze exceptions generated by GRC Process Control and assist clients in fine-tuning business rules.

Maintain master data, including creation of organizations, business processes, sub-processes, and controls according to company structure.

Configure GRC Process Control for integration with SAP BW.

Bachelor’s or Master’s Degree in Computer Science, Computer Engineering, Information Systems, or a related field.

Hands-on experience in SAP GRC Process Control (PC) and Access Control (AC) implementation and functional support.

Successfully completed at least one full-cycle SAP GRC PC/AC implementation, with practical experience in configuring: Risk Mapping, Role Management, Workflows, Risk Analysis, Continuous Control Monitoring, Business Rules, Risk Assessments, and Planner Tasks.

Demonstrated deep understanding of the SAP Controls Framework across end-to-end business processes including Supply Chain, Purchase-to-Pay, Record-to-Report, and Order-to-Cash.

Proven ability to collaborate with cross-functional teams to design, configure, and optimize GRC processes, controls, and workflows.

Strong technical skills to configure and execute SAP Access/Process Control (10.0 / 10.1 / 12.0) workflows.

In-depth knowledge of Risk Aggregation, Heat Maps, and Planner functionalities within GRC Process Control and GRC Risk Management.

Experience performing pre-installation and post-installation activities, including synchronizations, connectors, rule building, and MSMP workflow configuration.

Solid understanding of business processes, control frameworks, compliance requirements, regulations, and Segregation of Duties (SoD) principles within an SAP ERP environment.

Strong capability in configuring and implementing SAP GRC Access Control modules — ARA, EAM, ARM, BRM, and UAR.

Proven experience supporting SAP GRC solutions across Access Control (AC) and Process Control (PC).

Good understanding of SAP Security concepts, system landscape architecture, transport systems, and version compatibilities.

Good command of English (both written and spoken) with strong business communication and client interaction skills.
